title: eucryphia milliganii
intro: Eucryphia milliganii, also known as the 'Southern Copper Beech', is a small evergreen tree native to New Zealand. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine, and it is rich in compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Eucryphia milliganii has been found to have health benefits, including antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to protect against cell damage and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Eucryphia milliganii are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
